title: Today's AI Brief: OpenAI on AWS and Microsoft Build
summary: OpenAI models on AWS, Microsoft Build updates, and AI news

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your AI brief. Today, I'm looking at some significant developments in the AI space. OpenAI's frontier models and Codex are now available on AWS, and Microsoft had its Build 2026 event. Let's dive in. I have to say, I'm excited about these updates because they show how AI is becoming more accessible and integrated into our daily tech lives.

First up, OpenAI's frontier models and Codex are now generally available on AWS. This means enterprises can build with OpenAI using the AWS environments, controls, and procurement workflows they're already familiar with. It's a big deal because it gives companies a new path to integrate OpenAI's tech into their operations. I think of it like a familiar interface for businesses to tap into AI, making it easier for them to leverage these powerful tools.

Next, Microsoft had its Build 2026 event, which covered Windows, AI, and more. The event included a keynote presentation that introduced some new developments, including advancements in AI integration across Microsoft's products. What's interesting here is how Microsoft is focusing on making AI a seamless part of its offerings, which could lead to some really cool features and capabilities.

OpenAI frontier models and Codex are now available on AWS is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: OpenAI frontier models and Codex are now generally available on AWS, giving enterprises a new path to build with OpenAI through the AWS environments, controls, and procurement workflows they already use. Customers can get started with OpenAI on AWS and move faster from evaluation to production. The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

Microsoft Build 2026: All the news about Windows, AI, RTX Spark, and more, from theverge.com, is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: Tech Updated Today, Jun 2, 2026, 7:23 PM UTC Microsoft Build 2026: All the news about Windows, AI, RTX Spark, and more by Stevie Bonifield Microsoft Build 2026 kicked off with a keynote presentation that introduced some developer-focused Windows updates , an OpenClaw-based AI assistant called Scout , the new Majorana 2 quantum computing chip, and a Surface ... The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.

sources.title: OpenAI frontier models and Codex are now available on AWS
Microsoft Build 2026: All the news about Windows, AI, RTX Spark, and more
Microsoft Scout is a new AI personal assistant built on OpenClaw
